claim: A 4B model fine-tuned with RL using <$500 of compute outperformed a 235B model on tool-use financial analysis; bigger models aren't always the answer—tool discipline matters more than raw reasoning
actionable: Before scaling to larger models, try RL fine-tuning on smaller models with high-quality tool-use data; RL is often more cost-effective than model scaling
source_title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawford, Snorkel

claim: The Terence Tao effect: using an overly intelligent model to solve a constrained task adds unnecessary capability. A financial analyst doesn't need deep mathematical reasoning—they need reliable tool use
actionable: Match model capability to task requirements; avoid overkill models that add cost and latency without performance gains
source_title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawford, Snorkel

claim: The 235B model queried a non-existent table without inspecting available tables first; the fine-tuned 4B model learned to call get_table_names first, inspect schema, self-correct on errors
actionable: Larger models don't automatically learn tool discipline; explicitly train on the correct tool-use sequence (introspection before action, error handling)
source_title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawford, Snorkel

claim: Training on single-table examples only yielded the greatest uplift (13.9→26.6% on FinQA reasoning); tool discipline generalizes to harder multi-table problems without explicit multi-table training data
actionable: Start RL fine-tuning with simpler (single-table) scenarios; correct fundamental tool-use behaviors before introducing task complexity
source_title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawford, Snorkel

claim: Use eval rubrics (breaking down response rightness into multiple criteria) to diagnose failure modes before collecting training data; apply rubrics to identify behaviors to fix, then generate targeted RL data
actionable: Create detailed eval rubrics with yes/no criteria for each behavioral aspect; use rubric results to guide RL training data collection
source_title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawford, Snorkel

claim: Experts-in-the-loop data generation is critical for high-quality RL training; Snorkel uses PhD-level domain experts and industry practitioners to ensure training data quality
actionable: For critical RL training, hire domain experts to generate and verify training data; quality >> quantity
source_title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawford, Snorkel
